levels.authority: Defines the document-indexing objective, quality posture, and acceptable risk.
responsibilities: decide when quality is good enough for downstream scientific use
nondelegable_actions: accept a corpus-wide indexing workflow without spot-check evidence
change the scientific purpose of the index

levels.authority: Selects the source artifact, gate rules, and supervisor ticket.
nondelegable_actions: claim benchmark win without token and quality evidence
close a roadmap phase without synchronized evidence

levels.authority: Runs the bounded document-artifact audit and writes a report.
nondelegable_actions: edit tracked files unless the ticket explicitly authorizes it

levels.authority: Audits only the extracted source facts passed by the supervisor.
responsibilities: use only the allowed decision vocabulary

workspace.wrong_root_stop_rule: If the active workspace root does not resolve to the assigned project, write a blocked report and stop.

job.title: Strict document artifact audit
job.goal: Audit one document-indexing summary and preserve explicit source gate evidence in the supervisor decision.

forbidden_actions: create or modify GitHub issues or pull requests
copy raw transcripts into tracked files
claim ready_to_scale when source gate requires repair or supervisor audit
stop_conditions: required source artifact is missing or unreadable
active root is not the assigned workspace
auditor decision contradicts explicit source gate evidence
success_criteria: source facts are copied exactly into the report
auditor_decision is one of the allowed decision values
subagent payload excerpt is present and compact

public_safety.raw_transcript_policy: raw prompts, transcripts, provider details, and source text stay under ignored runtime paths
